mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns exceed 55 percent during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal income and stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary policy tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 or positive returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 55 percent of observations during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns fail to exceed 50 percent probability.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60 percent of windows when CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling housing starts indicates capital reallocation toward manufacturing and technology sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.015 or positive returns occur in fewer than 40 percent of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with rising real personal income confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in 55 percent of observations when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show quarter-over-quarter growth.
